ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: Create order succeeds when order date, customer, and one line item are provided
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates the single create-order business rule through the UI without crossing system boundaries.
preconditions: Application is deployed in the UAT environment and the Order Management module is accessible.
User account exists with role Sales_User and permission Create_Order in Order Management.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10045 and name John Smith.
Product record exists with Product Code SKU-1001 and description Wireless Mouse.
test_data: Line Item 1: Product Code SKU-1001, Quantity 2, Unit Price 25.00
steps: Open the Create Order page.
Enter order date 2026-04-10.
Select customer CUST-10045.
Add line item SKU-1001 with quantity 2.
Click the Save button.
expected_result: Order is created successfully. A new order number is displayed. The saved order contains order date 2026-04-10, customer CUST-10045, and one line item for SKU-1001.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates exception handling for an invalid request payload in a single API operation.
preconditions: UAT environment API is available.
Service account exists with permission to update order status via API.
Order ORD-20484 exists with current status Packed.
steps: Send a PATCH request to /api/orders/ORD-20484/status with an empty status value.
expected_result: HTTP 400 is returned. Response body contains a field-level validation error for status. Order ORD-20484 remains in status Packed.
review_feedback: completeness: Missing execution detail or contains quality violations

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Missing execution detail or contains quality violations

ac_text: Only valid state transitions are allowed.
title: API rejects unauthorized status update request for order ORD-20486
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates API-level rejection behavior for a single unauthorized update request.
preconditions: UAT environment API is available.
User token exists for role Read_Only_Analyst with no permission to update order status.
Order ORD-20486 exists with current status Packed.
steps: Send a PATCH request to /api/orders/ORD-20486/status with the read-only authorization token and status Shipped.
expected_result: HTTP 403 is returned. Response body indicates the caller is not authorized to update order status. Order ORD-20486 remains in status Packed.

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: UI prevents shipping a cancelled order from the order details screen
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates the single UI rule for an invalid status transition.
preconditions: UAT environment is deployed and accessible.
User account exists with role Operations_User and permission Order_Status_Update_UI.
Order ID ORD-45023 exists with current status Cancelled.
Order details page for ORD-45023 is available in the application.
steps: Open the order details page for Order ID ORD-45023.
Select Shipped from the status control.
Click the Save button.
expected_result: The application displays a validation message stating cancelled orders cannot move to shipped. The status is not changed and the page continues to show Cancelled for Order ID ORD-45023.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: Cancelled order remains blocked from shipping status update through API
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates a single business rule on the order status update API.
preconditions: UAT environment is deployed and accessible.
User account exists with role Operations_User and permission Order_Status_Update.
Order ID ORD-45021 exists with current status Cancelled.
Order history for ORD-45021 contains no prior Shipped status.
steps: Submit a status update request for Order ID ORD-45021 with status Shipped.
Capture the API response body.
Query the order record for Order ID ORD-45021.
expected_result: HTTP 409 is returned. Response body contains a business rule error indicating cancelled orders cannot move to shipped. Order ID ORD-45021 remains in Cancelled status in the database.

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: API rejects shipping update for cancelled order with validation error
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because the test validates rejection behavior for a single invalid status transition rule.
preconditions: UAT environment is deployed and accessible.
Service account exists with permission Order_Status_Update_API.
Order ID ORD-45022 exists with current status Cancelled.
API gateway is configured to allow authenticated status update requests.
steps: Submit an authenticated API request to update Order ID ORD-45022 to Shipped.
Capture the HTTP response.
Query the order record for Order ID ORD-45022.
expected_result: HTTP 409 is returned. Response body contains error code ORDER_STATUS_TRANSITION_INVALID. Order ID ORD-45022 remains Cancelled and no shipped timestamp is written.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: Security control blocks unauthorized shipped transition attempt on cancelled order
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates a single API security outcome related to the status update operation.
preconditions: UAT environment is deployed and accessible.
User account exists with role Read_Only_User and no permission Order_Status_Update_API.
Order ID ORD-45026 exists with current status Cancelled.
API authentication is enabled and valid credentials are available for the read-only user.
steps: Submit an authenticated API request to update Order ID ORD-45026 to Shipped using the read-only user token.
Capture the HTTP response.
Query the order record for Order ID ORD-45026.
expected_result: HTTP 403 is returned. Response body indicates insufficient permissions. Order ID ORD-45026 remains Cancelled and no status change is persisted.

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: UI disables shipped option for a cancelled order status change
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test checks a boundary UI condition for the same single status transition rule.
preconditions: UAT environment is deployed and accessible.
User account exists with role Operations_User and permission Order_Status_Update_UI.
Order ID ORD-45024 exists with current status Cancelled.
Status dropdown is configured to load all allowed and disallowed transitions for the order.
steps: Open the order details page for Order ID ORD-45024.
Expand the status dropdown.
Inspect the available status options.
expected_result: Shipped is not selectable for Order ID ORD-45024. The UI either hides Shipped or renders it disabled, and Cancelled remains the current status.

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: Database retains cancelled status after attempted shipped transition
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test verifies persistence behavior for one invalid state transition at the database layer.
preconditions: UAT database is accessible with read access for QA validation.
Application service account has permission to update order status records.
Order record ORD-45025 exists with status Cancelled in the orders table.
Audit table is enabled for order status changes.
steps: Execute the order status update transaction for Order ID ORD-45025 to Shipped.
Query the orders table for Order ID ORD-45025.
Query the audit table for Order ID ORD-45025.
expected_result: The orders table still shows Cancelled for ORD-45025. No shipped status row is committed, and the audit table records a rejected transition event or error entry instead of a successful status change.

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Order status change appears in reporting and notification feed for completed order update
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates the single acceptance criterion that status updates are visible in reporting and notifications within the Order Management module.
preconditions: Application is deployed in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Operations_User and has edit permission on Order Management and view permission on Reporting and Notifications.
Order ID ORD-10482 exists with current status Packed and customer ID CUST-20017.
Reporting dashboard is configured to display order status changes within the last 24 hours.
Notification center is configured to receive order status update events for ORD-10482.
steps: Open the Order Management screen for Order ID ORD-10482.
Select the status value Shipped.
Click the Save button.
Open the Reporting dashboard.
Open the Notifications center.
expected_result: The order status is saved as Shipped. The reporting dashboard shows ORD-10482 with status Shipped and an updated timestamp. The notifications center shows a new status update notification for ORD-10482 with status Shipped.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Reporting and notification records are created for a status update with a terminal state
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates the single acceptance criterion through the ETL path that publishes status updates to reporting and notifications.
preconditions: ETL job for order status events is scheduled and active in the UAT environment.
Source order event queue contains pending events for processing.
User account exists with role Operations_User and has permission to update order status.
Order ID ORD-45007 exists with current status In_Transit.
Reporting warehouse and notification sink are available for verification.
steps: Update Order ID ORD-45007 to status Delivered.
Trigger the ETL processing job.
Wait for ETL completion.
Query the reporting warehouse for ORD-45007.
Query the notification sink for ORD-45007.
expected_result: The ETL job completes successfully. Reporting contains ORD-45007 with status Delivered and the ETL processing timestamp. The notification sink contains a Delivered notification for ORD-45007.
review_feedback: data_integrity: Data integrity checks are weak

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: API status update is rejected when reporting and notification payload is missing required order identifier
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates rejection behavior for a single API rule related to status visibility events.
preconditions: Application API is deployed in the UAT environment.
User account or client credential exists with role Operations_API_Client and has permission to update order status.
Order status update endpoint is enabled for authenticated requests.
No existing order record is required for the invalid request payload.
steps: Send a POST request to the order status update endpoint with the invalid payload.
Capture the HTTP response.
Query the reporting event store for correlation ID CORR-77881.
Query the notification event store for correlation ID CORR-77881.
expected_result: HTTP 400 is returned. The response body contains a field-level validation error for orderId. No reporting event is created. No notification event is created for correlation ID CORR-77881.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Reporting and notification visibility remains correct when an order status is updated from a long status history
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates a boundary data variation in the UI for the same acceptance criterion.
preconditions: Application is deployed in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Operations_User and has edit permission on Order Management and view permission on Reporting and Notifications.
Order ID ORD-77840 exists with a status history of six prior changes.
Reporting dashboard supports filtering by order ID.
Notification center supports searching by order ID.
test_data: Previous statuses: Created, Packed, Shipped, Delivered, Refunded, Return_Approved
steps: Open the Order Management screen for Order ID ORD-77840.
Select the status value Closed.
Click the Save button.
Open the Reporting dashboard for ORD-77840.
Open the Notifications center for ORD-77840.
expected_result: The order status is saved as Closed. Reporting shows the full status history for ORD-77840 including the new Closed entry. Notifications show a new Closed status update without losing prior notifications.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Reporting and notifications reflect a rapid sequence of two valid status changes for the same order
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates a boundary data variation for a single acceptance criterion without crossing system boundaries.
preconditions: Application database is accessible in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Operations_User and has permission to update order status.
Order ID ORD-20991 exists with current status Packed.
Reporting tables store status history with millisecond timestamps.
Notification tables store one record per status change event.
steps: Update Order ID ORD-20991 to status Shipped.
Wait 2 seconds.
Update Order ID ORD-20991 to status Delivered.
Query the reporting status history for ORD-20991.
Query the notification records for ORD-20991.
expected_result: Two distinct status history entries exist for ORD-20991 in chronological order: Shipped followed by Delivered. Two notification records exist for ORD-20991, each matching one status change. The latest reporting entry shows Delivered.
review_feedback: data_integrity: Data integrity checks are weak

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Notification event is not duplicated when the same status update request is retried
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates exception handling for duplicate request processing within the same API feature.
preconditions: Application API is deployed in the UAT environment.
User account or client credential exists with role Operations_API_Client and has permission to update order status.
Order ID ORD-33015 exists with current status Packed.
Idempotency support is enabled for order status update requests.
Reporting and notification stores are available for verification.
steps: Send a POST request to update ORD-33015 to Shipped with Idempotency-Key 9f2c1d7e-3f7a-4f1a-9d8a-2b7d5c4a1101.
Resend the same POST request with the same Idempotency-Key.
Query the reporting store for ORD-33015.
Query the notification store for ORD-33015.
expected_result: The first request is processed successfully. The second request returns a duplicate-request response without creating a second status change. Reporting contains one Shipped entry for ORD-33015. Notifications contain one Shipped event for ORD-33015.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Reporting and notification updates are not created when the status update request is unauthorized
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates exception handling for unauthorized access within the API feature.
preconditions: Application API is deployed in the UAT environment.
Client credential exists with role ReadOnly_API_Client and no permission to update order status.
Order ID ORD-88012 exists with current status Packed.
Reporting and notification stores are available for verification.
Authentication is required for the order status update endpoint.
steps: Send a POST request to update ORD-88012 to Shipped using the ReadOnly_API_Client credential.
Capture the HTTP response.
Query the reporting store for ORD-88012 and correlation ID CORR-55109.
Query the notification store for ORD-88012 and correlation ID CORR-55109.
expected_result: HTTP 403 is returned. The response body indicates insufficient permissions. No reporting record is created. No notification record is created for ORD-88012 or correlation ID CORR-55109.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result
